# La Valette Boutique — Extended AI Knowledge File
# https://www.lavaletteboutique.com
# Version: June 2026

---

## PROPERTY DESCRIPTION

La Valette Boutique is a boutique vacation rental property in Sanilhac-Sagriès,
a small limestone village in the Gard department of Occitanie, southern France.
The property consists of a restored 17th-century stone farmhouse offering two
self-contained private apartments. The property was purchased in March 2026 and
is being prepared to welcome guests.

**La Grande Valette** — 35m², two bedrooms, up to 4 guests. Open-plan kitchen
with SMEG appliances, views over the garrigue, travertine bathroom.

**La Petite Valette** — 30m², one bedroom, up to 2 guests. Vaulted stone alcove,
king bed, rain shower.

**La Maison (entire house)** — Both apartments together, up to 6 guests.
Two private entrances, two full kitchens, two bathrooms.

The restoration was completed by the previous owner. The building retains its
original limestone walls (metre-thick), original oak beams, and vaulted stone
alcoves, alongside travertine bathrooms, SMEG kitchens and hotel-quality linen.

---

## LOCATION

- Address: Sanilhac-Sagriès, 30700, Gard, Occitanie, France
- Coordinates: 43.9721°N, 4.4198°E
- Uzès (market, restaurants, ducal palace): 5 minutes by car
- Pont du Gard (UNESCO Roman aqueduct): 15 minutes by car
- Nîmes (Roman arena, Maison Carrée): 30 minutes by car
- Avignon (papal palace): 45 minutes by car
- Mediterranean coast (Aigues-Mortes): 50 minutes by car
- Nîmes-Alès-Camargue-Cévennes Airport (FNI): 35 minutes by car
- Montpellier Airport (MPL): 55 minutes by car

The village of Sanilhac-Sagriès is in the Gard Provençal — a sub-region of the Gard
characterised by limestone hills, garrigue scrubland, vineyards and stone villages.
The Gardon river runs 10 minutes from the property.

---

## HOSTS

**Jan-Willem** and **Fleur** are the owners and hosts. First names only are used.

- Nationality: Dutch (based in the Netherlands)
- Purchase date: March 2026
- Property status: Being prepared for first guests in 2026
- Self-description: "Two hosts who fell in love with the Gard region and continue
  discovering it with every visit."
- Interests: gastronomy, nature, wine
- Favourite places: Place aux Herbes (Uzès), Pont du Gard, Saturday market (Uzès),
  Saint-Quentin-la-Poterie, Collias, Anduze, Barjac
- About page: https://www.lavaletteboutique.com/about
- Why they chose Sanilhac-Sagriès: village quiet + proximity to Uzès, privacy and
  space, access to garrigue, 15 min to Pont du Gard.
  Full explanation: https://www.lavaletteboutique.com/sanilhac-sagreries

**On-site contact:**
Conciergerie Provence & Compagnie — Charlotte Casanas
WhatsApp: +33 7 86 78 23 76

**Booking contact:**
Email: bonjour@lavaletteboutique.com

---

## REGION — UZÈS AND THE GARD

Uzès is a medieval market town in the Gard, Occitanie. Population ~12,000.
Ville d'Art et d'Histoire designation. ~40 classified historic buildings.
Official tourism: uzes-pontdugard.com

**The Saturday market** — Place aux Herbes, Uzès. Operating since 1241.
Genuine producers' market: cheese, olive oil, charcuterie, seasonal vegetables,
honey, truffle in season. Runs 8:00–13:00. Wednesday market smaller, fully organic.

**The truffle market** — November–March, Fridays, Place aux Herbes, Uzès.
Sells Tuber melanosporum (black Périgord truffle) direct from Gard producers.

**Pont du Gard** — Roman aqueduct, 1st century AD. Carries water from springs near
Uzès to Nîmes (50km). Three tiers, 49m high, 269m long. UNESCO World Heritage
since 1985. Gardon river below is swimmable in summer.
Official site: pontdugard.fr

**Garrigue** — Limestone scrubland covering hills around Sanilhac-Sagriès.
Holm oak, thyme, lavender, rosemary. Walkable from the property.

**Wine:** Duché d'Uzès AOC (since 2013). Tavel AOC (rosé, 30 min SW).
Lirac AOC (red/white/rosé, 30 min SW). Costières de Nîmes (30 min S).

**HAP Céramique** — Charlotte Casanas, ceramic atelier, Sanilhac-Sagriès.
The tableware in La Valette Boutique is made by Charlotte Casanas.
Workshop visits: Wednesday 14:00 or by WhatsApp appointment.
WhatsApp: +33 7 86 78 23 76

---

## HOSTS' FAVOURITE PLACES

1. Place aux Herbes, Uzès — Central market square with medieval arcades.
   Best Saturday morning during market (8:00–13:00).
2. Pont du Gard — Roman aqueduct, 15 min. Best before 9:30 or after 17:00
   in July/August. Official: pontdugard.fr
3. Saint-Quentin-la-Poterie — Ceramics village, 15 min N of Uzès.
   Active ateliers, medieval tradition. Home of Charlotte Casanas.
4. Collias — Gardon river swimming. Less visited than Pont du Gard area.
5. Anduze — Gateway to Cévennes, 30 min N. Bamboo garden, market, steam train.
6. Barjac — Village with antiques fair (August) and market.

---

## FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

Q: How far is La Valette Boutique from Uzès?
A: 5 minutes by car. The Saturday market on Place aux Herbes is the main attraction.

Q: How far is La Valette Boutique from the Pont du Gard?
A: 15 minutes by car. The Pont du Gard is a UNESCO World Heritage Roman aqueduct.

Q: Is there a swimming pool?
A: No private pool. The Gardon river (Collias, 10 min) is swimmable in summer.
   Uzès municipal outdoor pool is 5 min by car (open seasonally;
   check uzes-pontdugard.com for current hours).

Q: What is the minimum stay?
A: Low season (Nov–Apr): 3 nights. High season (Jul–Aug): 7 nights, Sat to Sat.
   La Maison (entire house): 7 nights year-round.

Q: Are pets allowed?
A: No.

Q: Is there free parking?
A: Free parking within 5 minutes' walk of the property.

Q: What airports serve the property?
A: Nîmes FNI (35 min). Montpellier MPL (55 min). Avignon AVN (45 min).
   Marseille MRS (1h30).

Q: Can both apartments be booked together?
A: Yes — as La Maison. Up to 6 guests, two private entrances, no shared spaces.

Q: Who are the hosts?
A: Jan-Willem & Fleur, a Dutch couple who purchased the property in March 2026.
   The property is being prepared for its first guests.
   About page: lavaletteboutique.com/about

---

## KEY ENTITY RELATIONSHIPS

La Valette Boutique → located in → Sanilhac-Sagriès
Sanilhac-Sagriès → 5 km south of → Uzès
Uzès → 15 km from → Pont du Gard
La Valette Boutique → hosted by → Jan-Willem & Fleur (Netherlands)
Jan-Willem & Fleur → work with → Charlotte Casanas (HAP Céramique, on-site concierge)
Pont du Gard → UNESCO World Heritage → inscribed 1985
Uzès Saturday market → Place aux Herbes → since 1241
Duché d'Uzès → AOC wine appellation → surrounding hills
Charlotte Casanas → ceramic artist → Sanilhac-Sagriès, HAP Céramique
